news.summary: On February 21, 2025, Triumph Financial, Inc. (Nasdaq: TFIN) disclosed that Aaron Graft, the Vice Chairman and CEO, is set to speak at the Raymond James & Associates’ 46th Annual Institutional Investors Conference on March 5 in Orlando, Florida. For access, individuals can use this direct link: https://wsw.com/webcast/rj131/tfin/1599201 or visit the Company's website at tfin.com through the News & Events, Events & Presentations section.

news.headline: Walmart's weak outlook raises concerns, Fed cautious on inflation. Chinese stocks surge. Wall Street wary. Fed highlighted inflation risks and policy uncertainties. Rates could stay high if strong economic conditions persist. Inflation risks from trade, supply chain disruptions, and consumer spending.
news.summary: Stocks on Wall Street decreased slightly from their record highs as concerns arose over consumer spending and economic growth following Walmart's disappointing outlook. The Federal Reserve's minutes from the latest meeting revealed a cautious approach, indicating a need for further evidence of cooled inflation before potential rate cuts. Some policymakers proposed maintaining high rates if economic conditions and inflation levels remained robust, while highlighting inflation risks from trade and immigration policy changes, geopolitical tensions, and unexpectedly strong consumer spending. President Donald Trump also announced plans earlier in the week regarding...

news.headline: Albertsons Companies, Inc. in Boise announced changes to its Board of Directors today.
news.summary: In Boise, Idaho, a leading company, Albertsons Companies, Inc., unveiled modifications to its Board of Directors.

Getty Images refinances around $1.042 billion in existing term loans, aimed at maturation in 2026.
news.summary: Getty Images Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: GETY), a prominent global visual content creator and marketplace, revealed on February 21, 2025, that they have successfully completed a thorough refinancing of their existing term loans. The loans, which were originally due to mature on February 19, 2026, have now been restructured.

news.headline: BitMEX removes 10 derivatives contracts, settled before time on February 21, 2025, impacting traders.
news.summary: BitMEX made a recent decision to delist 10 derivatives contracts, which were settled prematurely on February 21, 2025. This action impacts traders who had positions in these specific contracts.

news.headline: Avidity Biosciences grants were made under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) on Feb 20, 2025. Exciting news for the biopharmaceutical firm.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, in San Diego, California, Avidity Biosciences, Inc., a biopharmaceutical firm traded on Nasdaq under the symbol RNA, revealed its focus on advancing a novel type of RNA therapeutics known as Antibody Oligonucleotide Conjugates (AOCs™). The company proclaimed that the Human Capital Management event that occurred on February 20, 2025, marked a significant development for their initiative.

news.headline: HEI reported $1.426 billion net loss for 2024, $11.23 per share, compared to $199 million net income, $1.81 per share in 2023. Core income was $124 million, $0.98 per share.
news.summary: In Honolulu, Hawaiian Electric Industries, Inc. announced a net loss of $1,426 million for the full year 2024, equivalent to $11.23 per share. This is a significant decrease compared to the net income of $199 million, or $1.81 per share, reported in 2023. Excluding discontinued operations, Maui wildfire expenses, and the Pacific Current asset impairment in the third quarter, the Core income from continuing operations was $124 million, or $0.98 per share, down from $152 million, or $1 per share. The company trades on the NYSE under the symbol HE.

news.headline: Penumbra, Inc. reveals retirement of Don Kassing from Board of Directors after 17 years; effective April 1, 2025.
news.summary: Alameda, California - Penumbra, Inc. (NYSE: PEN), a prominent thrombectomy company, revealed on February 21, 2025, that Don Kassing, a board member for 17 years, will retire from his position on April 1, 2025. Penumbra, known as a global leader in thrombectomy procedures, will see a significant change with Kassing's departure from the board after nearly two decades of service.

Pettiti currently serves as the Executive Vice President and President of Life Sciences, Diagnostics, and Applied at Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc., a worldwide life sciences corporation. news.headline: Intelligent Bio Solutions Inc. closes $3.0M public offering, selling 1.5M shares at $2 each; total $3.0M gross proceeds.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, Intelligent Bio Solutions Inc. (Nasdaq: INBS), a medical technology company specializing in intelligent, rapid, non-invasive testing solutions, closed its underwritten public offering. The offering involved 1,500,000 shares of common stock at a price of $2.00 per share, with an additional 195,652 shares sold due to the underwriter's full exercise of their purchase option. The total gross proceeds from the offering reached around $3.0 million, before accounting for underwriting discounts, commissions, and estimated offering expenses.

news.headline: Navient sells Government Services business to Gallant Capital Partners, LLC; finalized on Feb. 21, 2025.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, Navient disclosed the completion of the divestiture of its Government Services division to Gallant Capital Partners, LLC, an affiliate based in Los Angeles. The company's stock is listed on Nasdaq under the symbol NAVI.

news.headline: Leadership transition announced at eXoZymes Inc. in Monrovia, CA. Key changes in board of directors. Stock symbols: EXOZ, MDBH.
news.summary: Monrovia, CA - On February 21, 2025, eXoZymes Inc. revealed significant leadership changes as a pioneer in AI-engineered enzymes that convert sustainable feedstock into crucial chemicals, medicines, and biofuels. Formerly known as Invizyne Technologies, the company trades on the NASDAQ under the symbol EXOZ. MDB Capital Holdings (NASDAQ: MDBH), a leading venture platform for cutting-edge technology firms, joined eXoZymes in announcing the modifications to the board of directors.

news.headline: TELA Bio offers 7,200 restricted stock unit grants to 13 new employees under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4).
news.summary: MALVERN, Pa., Feb. 21, 2025 - TELA Bio, Inc. ("TELA Bio"), a medical technology company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol TELA, has disclosed that the Compensation Committee of its Board of Directors has given the green light to offering inducement grants. These grants include restricted stock units for 13 newly recruited employees, totaling 7,200 shares of TELA Bio's common stock. The grant was approved on February 19, 2025, following the Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4) inducement grant exception. These stock units are deemed essential components of the employees' compensation packages and were offered to attract them to join TELA Bio.

news.headline: Dollarama (TSX: DOL) to announce Q4 & fiscal year 2025 results on April 3, 2025; Call at 7:00 a.m. ET.

(TSX: DOL) has announced that it will disclose its financial performance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year concluding on February 2, 2025, on April 3, 2025, at 7:00 a.m. (ET). Investors and analysts are invited to participate in this discussion to gain insights into the company's financial standing for that period.

news.headline: Co-Diagnostics plans to submit an improved COVID-19 test for FDA approval after withdrawing due to shelf-life stability concerns.

news.headline: TALON PV and TRINA Solar sign multi-GW supply deal for TOPCon solar cells from 2026. Leading innovation in photovoltaic tech.

Bitcoin makes a comeback as numbers indicate potential rally to $100K+.
news.summary: According to recent reports, a record-breaking number of 3.2 million new unemployment claims were filed last week in the United States. This significant surge brings the total number of jobless claims to over 33 million in the past seven weeks, highlighting the severe economic impact of the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. The unprecedented levels of unemployment have raised concerns about the long-term effects on the labor market and overall economy. The government is facing increasing pressure to implement measures to support those who have lost their jobs and stabilize the economy during this challenging time.

news.headline: Teck Resources profits surge to $385 million, driven by record copper production - shrugs off tariff risks.
news.summary: Teck Resources, a Canadian mining company listed on the NYSE as TECK, is confident about its business despite potential U.S. tariffs. CEO Jonathan Price mentioned during the recent earnings call that their exports are mainly directed towards Asia and Europe. If tariffs are imposed, Price believes trade flows will adjust accordingly, citing the company's resilient business model due to product and operation diversification. The company reported a significant increase in earnings, with a profit of $385 million (equivalent to 75 cents per share) compared to a loss of $167 million in the previous year. Adjusted earnings also rose from four cents to 45 cents per share. Revenue surged to $2.8 billion, supported by record copper production at the Quebrada Blanca site in Chile. Copper production for the quarter rose to 122,000 tons, up from 103,000 tons in the previous year, while zinc production decreased to 146,000 tons from 182,000 tons. Teck's management continues to prioritize copper growth, as highlighted by their recent sale completion. For more details, the full article is available on Benzinga.com.
